symptoms.symptom_name: Acute behavioural escalation immediately after a change to routine (delayed outing perceived as cancelled)
symptoms.onset: shortly after the outing was delayed

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute behavioural dysregulation from change to routine
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clear precipitant processed through a known deficit in abstract/temporal reasoning.
A clear external, understandable trigger argues against internally-driven psychosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe intellectual disability with impaired abstract/temporal reasoning (baseline)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute behavioural dysregulation from change to routine
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to process 'later' explains why a delay is experienced as a permanent loss.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: occult physical pain or acute medical driver
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Communication impairment means a medical problem may present only as behaviour change — raises, not lowers, suspicion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Property destruction and superficial self-injury (forearm abrasions)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute behavioural crisis with physical-safety risk
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Active self-injury and breakage with glass present define an immediate safety risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unresponsive to verbal de-escalation, prolonged beyond usual pattern

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute behavioural crisis with physical-safety risk
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Failure of behavioural measures marks a crisis needing urgent in-person response.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diaphoresis and tachypnoea observed by staff

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute behavioural dysregulation from change to routine
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sympathetic arousal proportional to distress; non-specific but consistent.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: occult physical pain or acute medical driver
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Could also reflect pain, infection or metabolic upset — non-specific, does not exclude a medical cause.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute behavioural dysregulation from change to routine
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured; consistent with distress but non-specific. Sealed measurement — the AI infers arousal from staff report, not a measured rate.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute behavioural dysregulation from change to routine
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured mild elevation attributable to distress; non-diagnostic and sealed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: occult physical pain or acute medical driver
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No fever/hypoxia lowers (but does not exclude) infection; clinician-measured and sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Occult medical screen negative — no fever, normal bowels, no dental/ear/urinary pain (pertinent negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: occult physical pain or acute medical driver
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring staff screen reduces probability of an occult driver, but cannot exclude it remotely without examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: No preceding loss of consciousness, tonic-clonic activity, or incontinence (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a preceding ictus makes post-ictal agitation very unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abdomen soft and non-tender; tympanic membranes clear; dentition intact (clinician examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: occult physical pain or acute medical driver
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: In-person exam excluded common occult pain triggers — precisely the assessment telehealth CANNOT perform, which is why in-person clearance is required.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Frustration-driven behavioural crisis on change to routine
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute behavioural dysregulation in severe intellectual disability
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these define an active behavioural emergency with physical-safety risk arising from a routine-change trigger processed through a cognitive deficit.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: occult physical pain or acute medical driver (to be screened, not assumed absent)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The negatives that argue against an occult medical driver — but several require in-person examination, so a remote negative screen is not a clearance.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person medical assessment for occult pain/medical driver (abdominal, dental, ear, urinary, delirium screen)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A new behaviour change in intellectual disability requires face-to-face examination to exclude a treatable cause; this cannot be done via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bedside glucose / basic obs by the attending in-person team
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Simple reversible contributors (hypo/hyperglycaemia, fever) should be checked by whoever attends in person.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine bloods / septic screen / neuroimaging on spec
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated up front for a clear frustration-triggered crisis with a negative screen; avoid over-investigation unless the in-person assessment finds a driver.
